name: Star Anise Beef-Rice Noodle Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Flat rice-stick noodles, soaked in water for 20 minutes
6 oz Sirloin steak, trimmed of fat and sliced into paper-thin slices
1/4 cup Fresh coriander leaves, coarsely chopped
2 Green onions, cut into 2-inch-long thin julienne slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Place ginger, shallots, and onion on a baking sheet; broil until charred.
2. In a stockpot, bring water to a boil. Add oxtails and beef shank; bring to a boil. Skim and discard scum from the surface of the stock.
3. Reduce heat to low. Add charred ingredients, star anise, cinnamon stick, and cloves. Simmer for 2 hours.
4. Remove meat with tongs and shred it from the shank. Return bone to the pot and simmer for another hour.
5. When soup is done, remove and discard bones. Strain stock through a fine-mesh sieve; add fish sauce and salt.
6. Keep warm.
7. In a separate pot, bring 3 quarts water to a boil. Add noodles and cook for 1 minute. Drain in a colander.
8. Divide noodles among 8 soup bowls (about 1-to-2-cup-size). Top each bowl with shredded cooked beef, sirloin steak slices, onion slices, and bean sprouts.
9. Ladle hot soup stock over noodles and beef to cover; top with fresh coriander and green onions.
10. Serve with a squeeze of lime and sliced chiles.
